reasoning: The informal statement asserts that any finite group G of order 200 has a normal Sylow 5-subgroup. The Lean signature takes a finite group G (via `[Fintype G]` and `[Group G]`) with `Fintype.card G = 200` and concludes `∃ N : Sylow 5 G, N.Normal`. The type `Sylow 5 G` in Mathlib is the standard formalization of "Sylow 5-subgroup of G" (a maximal 5-subgroup), and `N.Normal` corresponds to normality of the underlying subgroup. The existential `∃ N : Sylow 5 G, N.Normal` correctly captures "there exists a Sylow 5-subgroup that is normal". No hypotheses are added or dropped, no quantifier structure is altered, and the types match the informal content precisely.
